message:
fix(engine): harden demotion receipt preimage with length-prefixed fields, schema version, and evidence binding

Strengthens the cryptographic integrity of DemotionReceipt by replacing
the pipe-delimited preimage format with length-prefixed binary encoding
and including previously-excluded fields in the signature preimage.

demotion_rollback.rs:
- Add append_length_prefixed_bytes() and append_length_prefixed_str()
  helpers for canonical binary encoding that prevents delimiter confusion
  attacks
- DemotionReason::append_canonical_bytes(): serialize full reason variant
  with all fields (not just the category tag) into the preimage buffer
- DemotionEvidenceItem::append_canonical_bytes(): include artifact_hash,
  category, collected_at_ns, and summary in canonical form
- compute_preimage_bytes(): now accepts schema_version and evidence
  parameters; uses length-prefixed encoding for all string fields instead
  of pipe-delimited format; includes evidence items in the preimage so
  receipts commit to the exact evidence set
- content_hash(): now delegates to compute_preimage_bytes + appends
  signature bytes, unifying the hash commitment with the signature
  commitment (previously used a separate ad-hoc preimage)
- create() and verify_signature(): pass schema_version and evidence to
  compute_preimage_bytes

This is a breaking change to receipt signature format — existing receipts
signed under the old pipe-delimited preimage will not verify. New receipts
include the full evidence chain in their cryptographic commitment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
